result:
Findings, ordered by severity.

---

**1. High — Watch Loop animates a transform inside a still-scrollable viewport, producing a blank rail and fighting motion after any manual scroll (desktop and tablet >820px).**

- **Evidence:** `styles.css` `.watch-loop-viewport { overflow-x: auto; }` has no desktop override, so the rail stays user-scrollable while the animation is enabled. `watch-loop.js` `syncLoopCopies()` sizes clones with `const requiredWidth = viewport.clientWidth + sequenceWidth;` — it assumes `scrollLeft === 0` and nothing ever resets or accounts for the viewport's scroll position. `initWatchLoops()` gates autoplay only on `matchMedia("(max-width: 820px)")` and `prefers-reduced-motion`, not on hover/pointer capability, and there is no `scroll` pause reason in `addInteractionListeners()`.
- **Impact:** On desktop, the rail exposes a thin scrollbar (`scrollbar-width: thin` plus 1rem bottom padding), so users can and will scroll it. Once `scrollLeft > 0`, every animation cycle where `offset` approaches `sequenceWidth` pushes the rendered clones left of the visible window, and a full-height blank fog-colored gap sweeps in from the right edge — on the exact 1440×900 configuration QA covers. On touch devices wider than 820px (the tested 834×1112 iPad size), autoplay is enabled with no hover to pause it; a swipe scrolls `scrollLeft` while `pointerup` immediately resumes the transform animation, so momentum scroll and autoplay fight, and the same blank region appears. The scrollbar thumb also never reflects the animated position. The stated QA (desktop pause behavior, manual fallback) misses this because it never combines manual scroll with autoplay.
- **Reproduction / fix:** At 1440×900, drag the Watch rail's scrollbar (or shift-scroll) right by a card or two, move the pointer off the section, and watch one loop cycle. Fix direction: when a controller is enabled, set the viewport to `overflow-x: hidden` (restoring `auto` in `disable()`/`restoreManualRail()`), or reset `scrollLeft` to 0 and include `viewport.scrollLeft` in `requiredWidth`; additionally gate autoplay on `(hover: hover) and (pointer: fine)` rather than width alone so touch tablets get the manual rail.

**2. Medium — Small uppercase label text fails WCAG AA contrast on the light backgrounds.**

- **Evidence:** `styles.css` — the shared rule `.eyebrow, .section-title, .card-kicker, .work-meta, .press-preview-title { font-size: 0.74rem; … }` combined with `.section-title`/`.work-meta { color: var(--signal-coral) }` (`#f0645a`) renders at ≈2.9:1 against `--paper: #f6f4ee` and ≈2.3:1 against `--fog: #dddcd7` (the "Selected Archive" `.section-title` sits on fog). `.case-study-item span { color: var(--signal-moss) }` (`#4d9259`, 0.7rem) is ≈3.4:1 on paper. AA requires 4.5:1 at these sizes.
- **Impact:** Every work card's credit line (`.work-meta`), all coral section titles, and the "Challenge / What I shaped" labels are low-contrast for low-vision users on both language pages. QA only verified contact-submit contrast.
- **Fix direction:** Darken the coral and moss values used on light backgrounds (or swap these labels to `--ink`/`--ink-soft`), keeping the current values for use on `--stage` where they pass.

**3. Medium — With JavaScript disabled, the contact form navigates away to a raw API response and always submits an empty anti-spam timestamp.**

- **Evidence:** `en/index.html` / `zh/index.html` `form.contact-form` posts natively to `action="/api/contact"`; the success/error strings live only in `data-success`/`data-error` attributes consumed by the fetch handler in `main.js`. The hidden field `<input type="hidden" name="startedAt" value="" data-contact-started-at>` is populated only by JS (`startedAt.value = String(Date.now())`).
- **Impact:** No-JS users who submit leave the site and land on the endpoint's JSON body (or an error page); no localized status is ever shown. Because `startedAt` arrives empty — the signature of the timing-based bot check the field exists for — legitimate no-JS submissions are likely to be classified as spam, silently losing inquiries from the primary conversion path.
- **Reproduction / fix:** Disable JS, submit the form. Fix direction: have the endpoint answer non-`Accept: application/json` posts with an HTML confirmation or redirect back with a query flag, and treat a missing `startedAt` as "no-JS" rather than "bot" (the `website` honeypot still covers naive bots).

**4. Medium — With JavaScript disabled or failed, the showreel play button renders but does nothing; there is no playback path.**

- **Evidence:** `en/index.html` / `zh/index.html` — `<button data-showreel-play>` has no non-JS behavior, and the `<video data-showreel-video … preload="none">` element ships without a `controls` attribute; controls are added only in `playShowreel()` in `main.js`.
- **Impact:** No-JS visitors (and anyone whose module script fails to load) see a prominent, focusable "Watch reel" button that silently does nothing — worse than no affordance. The stated no-JS QA covers only Watch rail access, so this is a gap the checks miss, and it breaks the "showreel playback" requirement in that mode.
- **Fix direction:** Add a `<noscript>` block that hides `.hero-play-button` and reveals the video with native `controls` (or an equivalent static link to the MP4), and have JS remove the attribute on init.

**5. Medium — Press-card image failure handler collapses the card layout; press/Watch media are hotlinked from four third-party hosts.**

- **Evidence:** All `.press-preview-card` images use `onerror="this.parentElement.remove()"` (both pages). `.press-preview-card { grid-template-columns: 4rem minmax(0, 1fr); }` in `styles.css` — removing `.press-preview-image` leaves `.press-preview-copy` (which has `min-width: 0`) auto-placed into the 4rem first track. Sources hotlinked: `prod-img.taiwanplus.com`, `i.ytimg.com`, `v3-statics.mirrormedia.mg`, `www.verymulan.com` (also used for two Watch cards and two `work-image`s).
- **Impact:** If any third-party image 404s or the host adds hotlink protection — entirely outside this site's control, and invisible to the "no broken images" check, which only passes *today* — the card's title and source text get crushed into a ~64px column instead of degrading gracefully. Hotlinking also discloses every visitor's IP and user agent to those four external hosts on page load, and the Watch/Selected Works imagery for Tech Dreamers and PTS Taigi disappears entirely if the hosts change URLs.
- **Fix direction:** Self-host copies of the press/work thumbnails under `/assets/`, and replace `remove()` with a class that keeps the two-column grid (e.g., a placeholder background) so a failed image never reflows the text.

**6. Medium — The Chinese page declares `lang="zh"` instead of `lang="zh-Hant"`, and its archive body copy is untranslated English.**

- **Evidence:** `zh/index.html` line 2: `<html lang="zh">`, while the page's own hreflang/canonical metadata declares `zh-Hant`. Further down, all four `.archive-item` entries keep English `.archive-body` text (e.g., "Short-form web drama work across food, fantasy, comedy…", "Thirteen-episode Taiwanese drama series starring Ella Chen and Chris Wu.") and partially English `.work-meta` ("Chinese online platforms", "Taiwan science collaboration").
- **Impact:** Bare `lang="zh"` lets platforms without the listed TC fonts (notably Windows fallback) resolve Han-unified codepoints to Simplified-style glyphs, visibly wrong for a Traditional Chinese audience, and gives translation/screen-reader tooling an ambiguous language signal. The English archive paragraphs break the stated bilingual-output requirement on a user-visible section of the zh page.
- **Fix direction:** Change to `<html lang="zh-Hant">` and translate the four archive descriptions and mixed meta lines.
